question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onApplicationEvent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Ordered.LOWEST_PRECEDENCE` ordering of `onApplicationEvent()` — meaning it runs after all other listeners — to ensure that migrated `hedera.*` properties are injected after security-sensitive listeners have already read the environment, causing those listeners to use legitimate values while the application beans use attacker-overridden values?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_RECORD_ENTITY_PERSIST_TOKENBALANCES=false` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to disable token balance persistence, making the mirror node unable to accurately track token holdings and enabling the attacker to exploit stale balance data for fraudulent transfers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.entity.persist.itemizedTransfers=false` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to disable itemized transfer persistence, hiding explicit HBAR transfer details from the mirror node's records and enabling undetected fund movements?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: convertPropertyName()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a property name `hedera.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].uri` pointing to an attacker-controlled URI, causing `convertPropertyName()` to produce a valid `hiero.*` property that overrides the legitimate stream source, redirecting the importer to download fraudulent transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_CONSENSUSMODE=EQUAL` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to override the consensus mode from `STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK` to `EQUAL`, making all nodes equally weighted regardless of stake, allowing an attacker controlling a low-stake node to have disproportionate influence over which transactions are considered valid?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.retry.maxAttempts=0` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to override the retry configuration and prevent the importer from retrying failed record parsing, causing legitimate transactions (including fund transfers) to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onApplicationEvent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `onApplicationEvent()` does not check whether a migrated `hiero.*` property already exists before calling `propertySources.addAfter()`, allowing the attacker to inject a `hedera.*` property that creates a duplicate `hiero.*` entry in the property source chain, causing Spring to resolve the attacker's value instead of the legitimate one for operator key configuration?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_RECORD_ENTITY_PERSIST_SYNTHETICCONTRACTLOGS=false` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to disable synthetic contract log persistence, hiding EVM token transfer events from the mirror node and enabling undetected theft of fungible tokens via smart contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.entity.persist.tokens=false` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to disable token data persistence, making the mirror node unable to track token creation and transfers, enabling the attacker to exploit the blind spot for fraudulent token operations?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_STARTBLOCKNUMBER=-1` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to override the start block number and force the importer to restart from the very first available block, potentially causing re-processing of historical transactions and double-crediting of balances?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.block.enabled=true` combined with `hedera.mirror.importer.block.bucketName=<attacker-bucket>` into property sources, causing `migrate()` to enable block stream ingestion from an attacker-controlled bucket, injecting fraudulent block data that misrepresents fund balances?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: convertEnvironmentVariable()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_LEDGER_LEDGERID` to a crafted base64 value, causing `convertEnvironmentVariable()` to override the ledger ID used for block verification, allowing the attacker to make the mirror node accept blocks from a different (attacker-controlled) ledger as valid?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.entity.persist.transactionHash=false` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to disable transaction hash persistence, preventing the mirror node from detecting duplicate or replayed transactions, enabling replay attacks that double-spend funds?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_RETENTION_ENABLED=true` combined with `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_RETENTION_PERIOD=1s` as environment variables, causing `migrate()` to enable aggressive data retention that purges transaction records almost immediately, destroying evidence of fund movements and enabling undetected theft?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.entity.persist.files=false` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to disable file data persistence, preventing the mirror node from recording system file updates (including address book changes), allowing the attacker to exploit stale address book data to redirect consensus node communications?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onApplicationEvent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `onApplicationEvent()` processes property sources in their existing iteration order — which may include cloud config sources loaded before local sources — to inject a `hedera.*` property into a cloud config source that gets migrated and takes priority over a local `hiero.*` operator key configuration?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_RECORD_ENTITY_PERSIST_TRANSACTIONHASH=false` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to disable transaction hash tracking, removing the mirror node's ability to detect replayed transactions and enabling double-spend attacks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].credentials.accessKey` and `hedera.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].credentials.secretKey` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to override cloud storage credentials with attacker-controlled values, redirecting stream file downloads to a malicious bucket?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_RECORD_ENTITY_PERSIST_SYSTEMFILES=false` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to disable system file persistence, preventing the mirror node from recording updates to the fee schedule (file 0.0.111), allowing the attacker to exploit stale fee data to underpay transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.entity.persist.transactionSignatures=CRYPTOTRANSFER` into a property source, causing `migrate()` to override the transaction signature persistence configuration, potentially exposing private key material in the database that can be used to steal funds?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HEDERA_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_RECORD_ENTITY_PERSIST_NFTS=false` as an environment variable, causing `migrate()` to disable NFT persistence, making the mirror node unable to track NFT ownership changes, enabling the attacker to exploit the blind spot to fraudulently transfer NFTs without detection?
